guideline_references: RACGP guidance on the febrile immunosuppressed patient
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: symmetric polyarthritis with a malar rash and low fever mimics septic arthritis and facial cellulitis.
Important-not-to-miss: infection in an immunomodulated patient, and serious SLE complications (serositis/pleuritis, nephritis) must be screened.
Anchoring: 'known SLE flare' should not close off infection or systemic organ involvement.
Commission trap: de-novo oral prednisolone over chat in a febrile immunomodulated patient is unsafe.
Telehealth limits: joint/facial/chest examination and bloods (including urinalysis for nephritis) cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
